body_txt: The protagonist has just done something stupid that he shouldn't have done. Everyone who knows what he did has abandoned him. The protagonist looks to his faithful Fido for validation — dogs love their owners unconditionally, right? No, the dog lowers his head and leaves the room. In comedic examples, the dog, or another pet, may instead put their paws over their eyes and whine, as the animal equivalent of a Facepalm . In live action, this trick is accomplished by placing the animal's paws over their eyes, filming them removing them, then reversing the film to make it look as though they're covering their own eyes. Sub-Trope of Animal Reaction Shot . An animal who pulls this trick often is probably a Silent Snarker .
